Focus
One correct answer per question unless a page explicitly says otherwise.
Unit 2 note
Unit 2 note: du is a contraction such as de + le, not a partitive article.

Reader alignment

Characters: Wei, Amara, Mateo, Yuki, Madame Benali, Madame Sow.
Settings: boulangerie, apartment above it, classroom, La Fougère, Paris streets, market, dinner, and Bilan.
LAF1201

Unit 2 · Daily life above the boulangerie

Épisode 2 — Une famille au-dessus de la boulangerie

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
FaireAller àVouloirC’est / ce sont and negationArticles and countable objectsOrigin and source: de + le = duPreferencesTimeObjects and descriptionPlaces and activitiesPhrases in contextDaily-life capstonePossessives

Unit 2Vouloir

Daily life moves between the apartment, the boulangerie, La Fougère, the market, and the classroom.
Daily-life French: faire (to do/make), aller (to go), vouloir (to want), articles le/la/les, telling time, places and activities, and possessives (mon, ton, son…).
Focus — Conjugate vouloir and use it to express what you want — either a noun (je veux du café) or an action via infinitive (je veux partir). Drills the irregular stem alternation (veu-, voul-, veul-) and the politeness shift to je voudrais in service contexts.

1. Stating an intention Je ___ sortir ce soir. I want to go out tonight.

2. Inviting a peer Tu ___ venir avec nous au cinéma ? Do you want to come to the cinema with us?

3. Reporting what someone wants Pierre ___ un café. Pierre wants a coffee.

4. Group plan Nous ___ aller à la plage. We want to go to the beach.

5. Asking a group politely Vous ___ un thé ou un café ? Would you like tea or coffee?

6. Group desire (third person plural) Mes amis ___ partir tôt. My friends want to leave early.

7. Vouloir + noun (request at café) Je veux ___ croissant, s'il vous plaît. I'd like a croissant, please.

8. Vouloir + infinitive (intention) Je veux ___ à 22h. I want to leave at 10pm.

9. Casual invitation: "do you want…" « ___ aller au cinéma ce soir ? » Do you want to go to the cinema tonight?

10. Polite request — softer than "je veux" Je ___ un sac, s'il vous plaît. I'd like a bag, please.

11. Negation with vouloir Non, je ___ pas sortir ce soir. No, I don't want to go out tonight.

12. Question with veux Qu'est-ce que tu ___ faire ? What do you want to do?

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

Focus
Épisode 2 — Une famille au-dessus de la boulangerie
Focus
Daily-life French: faire (to do/make), aller (to go), vouloir (to want), articles le/la/les, telling time, places and activities, and possessives (mon, ton, son…).
Focus
One correct answer per question unless a page explicitly says otherwise.
Unit 2 note
Unit 2 note: du is a contraction such as de + le, not a partitive article.

Reader alignment

Characters: Wei, Amara, Mateo, Yuki, Madame Benali, Madame Sow.
Settings: boulangerie, apartment above it, classroom, La Fougère, Paris streets, market, dinner, and Bilan.
LAF1201

Unit 2 · Daily life above the boulangerie

Épisode 2 — Une famille au-dessus de la boulangerie

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
FaireAller àVouloirC’est / ce sont and negationArticles and countable objectsOrigin and source: de + le = duPreferencesTimeObjects and descriptionPlaces and activitiesPhrases in contextDaily-life capstonePossessives

Unit 2C’est / ce sont and negation

Daily life moves between the apartment, the boulangerie, La Fougère, the market, and the classroom.
Daily-life French: faire (to do/make), aller (to go), vouloir (to want), articles le/la/les, telling time, places and activities, and possessives (mon, ton, son…).
Focus — Identify things with c'est / ce sont and negate them: ce n'est pas, ce ne sont pas. Tests number agreement (singular vs plural identification) and the trickier ce → c' elision before a vowel.

1. Identifying one thing « ___ un livre. » It's a book.

2. Identifying multiple things « ___ des livres. » They are books.

3. Denying singular identification Non, ___ un stylo, c'est un crayon. No, it's not a pen, it's a pencil.

4. Denying plural identification Non, ___ des stylos, ce sont des crayons. No, they're not pens, they're pencils.

5. Cessation: "not anymore" Avant, je faisais du sport, mais maintenant je ___ de sport. I used to do sport, but now I don't do sport anymore.

6. Cessation in different verb Avant, j'étais étudiant, mais maintenant je ___ étudiant. I used to be a student, but now I'm not a student anymore.

8. Asking what something is « ___ ? — C'est mon sac. » What is it? — It's my bag.

9. Possessive identification « ___ livre, pas le tien. » It's MY book, not yours.

10. Correcting ownership « ___ mon sac, c'est le sac de Marc. » It's not my bag, it's Marc's bag.

11. C'est + masculine noun starting with vowel « Regarde ! ___ éléphant ! » Look! It's an elephant!

12. How do you say not anymore Je n'aime ___ le café maintenant — j'ai changé. I don't like coffee anymore — I've changed.

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

Focus
Épisode 2 — Une famille au-dessus de la boulangerie
Focus
Daily-life French: faire (to do/make), aller (to go), vouloir (to want), articles le/la/les, telling time, places and activities, and possessives (mon, ton, son…).
Focus
One correct answer per question unless a page explicitly says otherwise.
Unit 2 note
Unit 2 note: du is a contraction such as de + le, not a partitive article.

Reader alignment

Characters: Wei, Amara, Mateo, Yuki, Madame Benali, Madame Sow.
Settings: boulangerie, apartment above it, classroom, La Fougère, Paris streets, market, dinner, and Bilan.
LAF1201

Unit 2 · Daily life above the boulangerie

Épisode 2 — Une famille au-dessus de la boulangerie

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
FaireAller àVouloirC’est / ce sont and negationArticles and countable objectsOrigin and source: de + le = duPreferencesTimeObjects and descriptionPlaces and activitiesPhrases in contextDaily-life capstonePossessives

Unit 2Articles and countable objects

Daily life moves between the apartment, the boulangerie, La Fougère, the market, and the classroom.
Daily-life French: faire (to do/make), aller (to go), vouloir (to want), articles le/la/les, telling time, places and activities, and possessives (mon, ton, son…).
Focus — Pick the right definite or indefinite article (le/la/les, un/une/des) for everyday objects. Drills two choices at once: definite vs indefinite (the specific cup vs a cup) and gender + number on the noun. Think of definite articles as bounded — pointing at the whole known thing (le cinéma = cinema as a concept) — and indefinite articles as introducing one of many (un livre = some book, not yet specific).

1. Identifying a masculine object Dans mon sac, il y a ___ livre. In my bag, there's a book.

2. Identifying a feminine object Dans mon sac, il y a ___ trousse. In my bag, there's a pencil case.

3. Multiple objects Dans mon sac, il y a ___ stylos. In my bag, there are pens.

4. Vowel-starting masculine noun J'ai ___ agenda dans mon sac. I have a planner in my bag.

5. Existence: il y a (singular) Sur la table, ___ une pomme. On the table, there is an apple.

6. Existence: il y a (plural) Sur la table, ___ trois pommes. On the table, there are three apples.

7. Indefinite article + number J'ai ___ feuilles. I have two sheets.

8. Negation collapses indefinite to de Dans mon sac, il n'y a pas ___ livre. In my bag, there's no book.

9. Vowel-starting noun under negation: d' Il n'y a pas ___ parapluie ici. There's no umbrella here.

10. Exception: c'est keeps un/une Non, ce n'est pas ___ livre, c'est un cahier. No, it's not a book, it's a notebook.

11. How do you say "Is there a computer in your room" « ___ un ordinateur dans ta chambre ? » Is there a computer in your room?

12. Plural agreement issue: "books, pens, and a notebook" Dans mon sac, il y a ___ livres, des stylos et une trousse. In my bag, there are books, pens, and a pencil case.

13. Definite article + day (habitual) ___ lundi, j'ai cours de français. On Mondays, I have French class.

14. Definite article + feminine noun On va à ___ boulangerie de Madame Benali. We're going to Madame Benali's bakery.

15. Definite article + plural noun ___ étudiants sont en classe. The students are in class.

16. Definite article before vowel Nous habitons dans ___ appartement au-dessus. We live in the apartment above.

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

Focus
Épisode 2 — Une famille au-dessus de la boulangerie
Focus
Daily-life French: faire (to do/make), aller (to go), vouloir (to want), articles le/la/les, telling time, places and activities, and possessives (mon, ton, son…).
Focus
One correct answer per question unless a page explicitly says otherwise.
Unit 2 note
Unit 2 note: du is a contraction such as de + le, not a partitive article.

Reader alignment

Characters: Wei, Amara, Mateo, Yuki, Madame Benali, Madame Sow.
Settings: boulangerie, apartment above it, classroom, La Fougère, Paris streets, market, dinner, and Bilan.
LAF1201

Unit 2 · Daily life above the boulangerie

Épisode 2 — Une famille au-dessus de la boulangerie

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
FaireAller àVouloirC’est / ce sont and negationArticles and countable objectsOrigin and source: de + le = duPreferencesTimeObjects and descriptionPlaces and activitiesPhrases in contextDaily-life capstonePossessives

Unit 2Origin and source: de + le = du

Daily life moves between the apartment, the boulangerie, La Fougère, the market, and the classroom.
Daily-life French: faire (to do/make), aller (to go), vouloir (to want), articles le/la/les, telling time, places and activities, and possessives (mon, ton, son…).
Focus — Apply the de + article contractions for origin, source, and possession: de + le = du, de + les = des, de la and de l' stay as is. The mirror image of 2-02's à + article set — same logic, different preposition. The contraction keeps the article's bounded force intact: du cinéma still points at a specific, known whole — it's de + le fused, not vagueness.

1. Partitive: masculine consonant Je mange ___ pain. I eat (some) bread.

2. Partitive: feminine consonant Tu manges ___ salade ? Are you eating (some) salad?

3. Partitive: vowel-starting Je bois ___ eau. I drink (some) water.

4. Partitive: plural Je commande ___ frites. I'm ordering (some) fries.

5. Partitive in negation: collapse to de Je ne mange pas ___ pain. I don't eat bread.

6. Partitive in negation: vowel-start Je ne bois pas ___ eau. I don't drink water.

7. Partitive in negation: plural Je ne mange pas ___ frites. I don't eat fries.

8. Activity: faire + partitive Je fais ___ natation deux fois par semaine. I swim twice a week.

9. Negation of sport activity Je ne fais pas ___ natation. I don't swim.

10. Partitive vs. à-contraction (lookalikes) Je vais ___ cinéma ce soir. I'm going to the cinema tonight.

11. Partitive with abstract noun Tu as ___ temps demain ? Do you have (some) time tomorrow?

12. Partitive vs. definite — the aimer trap J'aime ___ café. I like coffee.

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

Focus
Épisode 2 — Une famille au-dessus de la boulangerie
Focus
Daily-life French: faire (to do/make), aller (to go), vouloir (to want), articles le/la/les, telling time, places and activities, and possessives (mon, ton, son…).
Focus
One correct answer per question unless a page explicitly says otherwise.
Unit 2 note
Unit 2 note: du is a contraction such as de + le, not a partitive article.

Reader alignment

Characters: Wei, Amara, Mateo, Yuki, Madame Benali, Madame Sow.
Settings: boulangerie, apartment above it, classroom, La Fougère, Paris streets, market, dinner, and Bilan.
LAF1201

Unit 2 · Daily life above the boulangerie

Épisode 2 — Une famille au-dessus de la boulangerie

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
FaireAller àVouloirC’est / ce sont and negationArticles and countable objectsOrigin and source: de + le = duPreferencesTimeObjects and descriptionPlaces and activitiesPhrases in contextDaily-life capstonePossessives

Unit 2Preferences

Daily life moves between the apartment, the boulangerie, La Fougère, the market, and the classroom.
Daily-life French: faire (to do/make), aller (to go), vouloir (to want), articles le/la/les, telling time, places and activities, and possessives (mon, ton, son…).
Focus — Express liking and preference with aimer, adorer, préférer, détester, and crucially the definite article rule that follows them — j'aime LE café (in general), not j'aime du café. The contrast with eating/drinking verbs is what the drill keeps testing.

1. Liking + noun (definite article) J'aime ___ café. I like coffee.

2. Liking with feminine noun J'adore ___ musique classique. I love classical music.

3. Liking with plural J'aime ___ chats. I like cats.

4. Detesting Je déteste ___ brocoli. I hate broccoli.

5. Preference + infinitive (verb) J'aime ___ avec mes amis. I like dancing with my friends.

6. Detesting an activity Je déteste ___ le soir. I hate cooking in the evening.

7. Negation: pas de DOESN'T apply Je n'aime pas ___ café. I don't like coffee.

8. Contrast with "mais" J'aime le thé, ___ je n'aime pas le café. I like tea, but I don't like coffee.

9. Préférer + comparison Je préfère le cinéma ___ théâtre. I prefer cinema to theatre.

10. Asking about preferences « ___ aimes le café ? » Do you like coffee?

11. Multiple likes joined by et J'aime le sport ___ la musique. I like sport and music.

12. Strength comparison J' ___ la musique — c'est ma passion ! I love music — it's my passion!

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

Focus
Épisode 2 — Une famille au-dessus de la boulangerie
Focus
Daily-life French: faire (to do/make), aller (to go), vouloir (to want), articles le/la/les, telling time, places and activities, and possessives (mon, ton, son…).
Focus
One correct answer per question unless a page explicitly says otherwise.
Unit 2 note
Unit 2 note: du is a contraction such as de + le, not a partitive article.

Reader alignment

Characters: Wei, Amara, Mateo, Yuki, Madame Benali, Madame Sow.
Settings: boulangerie, apartment above it, classroom, La Fougère, Paris streets, market, dinner, and Bilan.
LAF1201

Unit 2 · Daily life above the boulangerie

Épisode 2 — Une famille au-dessus de la boulangerie

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
FaireAller àVouloirC’est / ce sont and negationArticles and countable objectsOrigin and source: de + le = duPreferencesTimeObjects and descriptionPlaces and activitiesPhrases in contextDaily-life capstonePossessives

Unit 2Time

Daily life moves between the apartment, the boulangerie, La Fougère, the market, and the classroom.
Daily-life French: faire (to do/make), aller (to go), vouloir (to want), articles le/la/les, telling time, places and activities, and possessives (mon, ton, son…).
Focus — Tell time with il est + heure for the current time, and c'est à + heure for scheduled events. Drills the il est vs c'est à contrast plus the half-hour and quarter-hour formulas (et demie, et quart, moins le quart).

1. Stating current time « Quelle heure est-il ? — ___ dix heures. » What time is it? — It's ten o'clock.

2. Asking about a scheduled event « Le film, ___ quelle heure ? » The film, what time is it (scheduled)?

3. Half past Il est deux heures ___ . It's half past two.

4. Quarter past Il est quatre heures ___ . It's a quarter past four.

5. Quarter to Il est neuf heures ___ . It's a quarter to nine.

6. Minutes past Il est dix heures ___ . It's twenty past ten.

7. Minutes before Il est huit heures ___ . It's ten to eight.

8. Noon and midnight Il est ___ , on mange ! It's noon, let's eat!

9. Time of day specifier Il est huit heures ___ . It's 8 in the evening.

10. When does class start? (event scheduling) Le cours, ___ neuf heures du matin. Class is at 9 in the morning.

11. Asking when something starts « ___ tu commences le travail ? » What time do you start work?

12. Schedule with mais and time Le film c'est à 20h, mais le concert, ___ 21h30. The film is at 8pm, but the concert is at 9:30pm.

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

Focus
Épisode 2 — Une famille au-dessus de la boulangerie
Focus
Daily-life French: faire (to do/make), aller (to go), vouloir (to want), articles le/la/les, telling time, places and activities, and possessives (mon, ton, son…).
Focus
One correct answer per question unless a page explicitly says otherwise.
Unit 2 note
Unit 2 note: du is a contraction such as de + le, not a partitive article.

Reader alignment

Characters: Wei, Amara, Mateo, Yuki, Madame Benali, Madame Sow.
Settings: boulangerie, apartment above it, classroom, La Fougère, Paris streets, market, dinner, and Bilan.
LAF1201

Unit 2 · Daily life above the boulangerie

Épisode 2 — Une famille au-dessus de la boulangerie

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
FaireAller àVouloirC’est / ce sont and negationArticles and countable objectsOrigin and source: de + le = duPreferencesTimeObjects and descriptionPlaces and activitiesPhrases in contextDaily-life capstonePossessives

Unit 2Objects and description

Daily life moves between the apartment, the boulangerie, La Fougère, the market, and the classroom.
Daily-life French: faire (to do/make), aller (to go), vouloir (to want), articles le/la/les, telling time, places and activities, and possessives (mon, ton, son…).
Focus — Describe everyday objects by combining noun + adjective with correct agreement: un sac noir / une voiture noire / des sacs noirs. Drills the four-form pattern — masculine/feminine, singular/plural — across a wider adjective range than 0-08's colours.

1. Identifying a masculine object « Qu'est-ce que c'est ? — ___ sac. » What is it? — It's a bag.

2. Feminine object « C'est ___ trousse. » It's a pencil case.

3. Adjective agreement (m. → f.) C'est un sac ___ . It's a red bag.

4. Adjective agreement (f. with -e added) C'est une trousse ___ . It's a green pencil case.

5. Position: petit before, rouge after C'est un ___ sac. It's a small bag.

6. Two adjectives: BANGS before + colour after C'est un ___ livre rouge. It's a small red book.

7. Possessive: mon (m.) C'est ___ sac. It's my bag.

8. Possessive: ma (f.) C'est ___ trousse. It's my pencil case.

9. Possessive: mes (plural) Ce sont ___ stylos. They are my pens.

10. Ownership with "de" C'est ___ Marc. It's Marc's pen.

11. Confirming ownership question « C'est ___ sac ? » Is this your bag?

12. Correcting ownership Non, ce n'est pas mon sac, ___ Marc. No, it's not my bag, it's Marc's bag.

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

Focus
Épisode 2 — Une famille au-dessus de la boulangerie
Focus

Focus
One correct answer per question unless a page explicitly says otherwise.
Unit 2 note
Unit 2 note: du is a contraction such as de + le, not a partitive article.

Reader alignment

Characters: Wei, Amara, Mateo, Yuki, Madame Benali, Madame Sow.
Settings: boulangerie, apartment above it, classroom, La Fougère, Paris streets, market, dinner, and Bilan.
LAF1201

Unit 2 · Daily life above the boulangerie

Épisode 2 — Une famille au-dessus de la boulangerie

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
FaireAller àVouloirC’est / ce sont and negationArticles and countable objectsOrigin and source: de + le = duPreferencesTimeObjects and descriptionPlaces and activitiesPhrases in contextDaily-life capstonePossessives

Unit 2Places and activities

Daily life moves between the apartment, the boulangerie, La Fougère, the market, and the classroom.
Daily-life French: faire (to do/make), aller (to go), vouloir (to want), articles le/la/les, telling time, places and activities, and possessives (mon, ton, son…).
Focus — Map an activity to its location with aller à and faire de: aller à la piscine / faire de la natation, aller au cinéma / regarder un film. Drills activity ↔ place pairing and reuses the contractions from 2-02 and 2-06.

1. Going to the cinema Ce soir, je vais ___ voir un film. Tonight, I'm going to the cinema to see a film.

2. At the swimming pool Le mardi, je nage ___ . On Tuesdays, I swim at the pool.

3. At the beach L'été, je vais ___ . In summer, I go to the beach.

4. At the library Pour étudier, je vais ___ . To study, I go to the library.

5. At the restaurant Samedi soir, je mange ___ avec mes amis. Saturday evening, I'm eating at the restaurant with my friends.

6. At the café On prend un verre ___ ? Shall we have a drink at the café?

7. At the hospital (vowel start) Mon ami est ___ . My friend is at the hospital.

8. Activity matching: theatre Pour voir une pièce, on va ___ . To see a play, we go to the theatre.

9. Activity matching: museum Au musée, je ___ une exposition. At the museum, I see an exhibition.

10. Activity matching: train station Pour aller à Lyon, je vais ___ prendre le train. To go to Lyon, I'm going to the station to take the train.

11. School / university Du lundi au vendredi, je vais ___ . Monday to Friday, I go to university.

12. Multi-activity at home Je reste ___ ce weekend. I'm staying home this weekend.

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Focus

Unit 2 note
Unit 2 note: du is a contraction such as de + le, not a partitive article.

LAF1201

Unit 2 · Daily life above the boulangerie

Épisode 2 — Une famille au-dessus de la boulangerie

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
FaireAller àVouloirC’est / ce sont and negationArticles and countable objectsOrigin and source: de + le = duPreferencesTimeObjects and descriptionPlaces and activitiesPhrases in contextDaily-life capstonePossessives

Unit 2Phrases in context

Daily life moves between the apartment, the boulangerie, La Fougère, the market, and the classroom.
Daily-life French: faire (to do/make), aller (to go), vouloir (to want), articles le/la/les, telling time, places and activities, and possessives (mon, ton, son…).
Focus — Choose the phrase that best fits the described situation.

1. Greeting a shop owner in the morning Vous entrez dans une boulangerie le matin. Vous dites : You enter a bakery in the morning. You say:

2. Asking politely for bread at a bakery Vous voulez acheter du pain. Vous dites : You want to buy bread. You say:

3. Saying goodbye to friends informally Vous quittez vos amis après les cours. Vous dites : You leave your friends after class. You say:

4. Asking someone to repeat what they said Vous n'avez pas compris. Vous dites : You didn't understand. You say:

5. Accepting an invitation Un ami vous invite à dîner. Vous acceptez : A friend invites you to dinner. You accept:

6. Declining politely On vous propose une sortie mais vous êtes occupé. Vous dites : Someone suggests an outing but you're busy. You say:

7. Asking for the price Vous voulez savoir le prix d'un article. Vous demandez : You want to know the price of an item. You ask:

8. Thanking someone for help Quelqu'un vous aide avec vos sacs. Vous dites : Someone helps you with your bags. You say:

9. Apologising for being late Vous arrivez en retard à un rendez-vous. Vous dites : You arrive late to a meeting. You say:

10. Offering help Vous voyez quelqu'un perdu dans la rue. Vous proposez : You see someone lost in the street. You offer:

11. Asking where something is Vous cherchez la gare. Vous demandez à un passant : You're looking for the train station. You ask a passerby:

12. Ordering at a restaurant Le serveur arrive. Vous commandez : The waiter arrives. You order:

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

Focus
Épisode 2 — Une famille au-dessus de la boulangerie
Focus
Daily-life French: faire (to do/make), aller (to go), vouloir (to want), articles le/la/les, telling time, places and activities, and possessives (mon, ton, son…).
Focus
One correct answer per question unless a page explicitly says otherwise.
Unit 2 note
Unit 2 note: du is a contraction such as de + le, not a partitive article.

Reader alignment

Characters: Wei, Amara, Mateo, Yuki, Madame Benali, Madame Sow.
Settings: boulangerie, apartment above it, classroom, La Fougère, Paris streets, market, dinner, and Bilan.
LAF1201

Unit 2 · Daily life above the boulangerie

Épisode 2 — Une famille au-dessus de la boulangerie

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
FaireAller àVouloirC’est / ce sont and negationArticles and countable objectsOrigin and source: de + le = duPreferencesTimeObjects and descriptionPlaces and activitiesPhrases in contextDaily-life capstonePossessives

Unit 2Daily-life capstone

Daily life moves between the apartment, the boulangerie, La Fougère, the market, and the classroom.
Daily-life French: faire (to do/make), aller (to go), vouloir (to want), articles le/la/les, telling time, places and activities, and possessives (mon, ton, son…).
Focus — A single plot, twelve moves: someone wants to go to the cinema on Saturday. The invitation goes out (vouloir), the destination is set (aller au cinéma), the time is named (il est / c'est à), a conflict appears (negation), articles drop out, preferences clash, the order shifts to pizza, the rendezvous gets locked in, and finally the whole plan is restated as one clean sentence. The narrative is invitation → conflict → reschedule → confirmation — what you're really being tested on is whether you can keep the same plan in your head while the grammar shifts under it.

1. Drawing on Skill 03 (vouloir) « ___ venir au cinéma ce soir ? » Do you want to come to the cinema tonight?

2. Drawing on Skills 02 + 10 On va ___ ce soir. We're going to the cinema tonight.

3. Drawing on Skill 08 (time) Le film, ___ 20h. The film is at 8pm.

4. Drawing on Skill 04 (negation) Désolé, je ___ libre ce soir. Sorry, I'm not free anymore tonight.

5. Drawing on Skills 05 + 06 (articles) Je n'ai pas ___ temps demain. I don't have time tomorrow.

6. Drawing on Skill 07 (preferences) Je n'aime pas ___ films d'horreur. I don't like horror films.

7. Drawing on Skill 03 (vouloir + politeness) « Bonjour, je ___ une pizza, s'il vous plaît. » Hello, I'd like a pizza, please.

8. Drawing on Skill 11 (functional writing) « ___ demain à 19h, à+ ! » Meet up tomorrow at 7pm, see you later!

9. Drawing on Skills 01 + 06 Je ___ sport ces jours-ci. I'm not doing any sport these days.

10. Drawing on Skills 02 + 03 + 10 « Tu ___ à la piscine samedi ? » Do you want to go to the pool Saturday?

11. Drawing on Skills 04 + 08 + 11 Désolé, ___ à 20h. Vers 21h ? Sorry, 8pm doesn't work for me. How about 9pm?

12. Full integration: making the plan « Samedi soir, ___ au cinéma à 20h. » Saturday evening, we are going to the cinema at 8pm.

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

Focus
Épisode 2 — Une famille au-dessus de la boulangerie
Focus
Daily-life French: faire (to do/make), aller (to go), vouloir (to want), articles le/la/les, telling time, places and activities, and possessives (mon, ton, son…).
Focus
One correct answer per question unless a page explicitly says otherwise.
Unit 2 note
Unit 2 note: du is a contraction such as de + le, not a partitive article.

Reader alignment

Characters: Wei, Amara, Mateo, Yuki, Madame Benali, Madame Sow.
Settings: boulangerie, apartment above it, classroom, La Fougère, Paris streets, market, dinner, and Bilan.
LAF1201

Unit 2 · Daily life above the boulangerie

Épisode 2 — Une famille au-dessus de la boulangerie

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
FaireAller àVouloirC’est / ce sont and negationArticles and countable objectsOrigin and source: de + le = duPreferencesTimeObjects and descriptionPlaces and activitiesPhrases in contextDaily-life capstonePossessives

Unit 2Possessives

Daily life moves between the apartment, the boulangerie, La Fougère, the market, and the classroom.
Daily-life French: faire (to do/make), aller (to go), vouloir (to want), articles le/la/les, telling time, places and activities, and possessives (mon, ton, son…).
Focus — Pick the right possessive adjective (mon, ma, mes, ton, ta, tes, son, sa, ses, notre, votre, leur(s)). The trap drilled hardest: French agrees with the noun's gender, not the owner's — sa voiture can mean "his car" or "her car" depending on context.

1. My + masculine singular noun J'ai oublié ___ livre chez moi. I left my book at home.

2. My + feminine singular noun Je voudrais vous présenter ___ famille. I'd like to introduce my family.

4. Your (informal) + feminine noun Comment va ___ copine ? How is your girlfriend/friend?

5. His/her + masculine noun Pierre a posé ___ sac sur la chaise. Pierre put his bag on the chair.

6. His/her + feminine noun Marie adore ___ ville natale. Marie loves her hometown.

7. My + plural noun J'ai perdu ___ clés ! I've lost my keys!

8. Your (informal) + plural noun Tu as pris ___ affaires ? Did you take your things?

9. His/her + plural noun Luc prend ___ affaires et part. Luc takes his things and leaves.

10. Before a vowel: feminine noun Je te présente ___ amie Camille. Let me introduce my friend Camille.

11. Before a vowel: his/her feminine noun Il cherche ___ ancienne école. He's looking for his old school.

12. Son/sa/ses — ambiguous owner Je n'ai pas ___ adresse email. I don't have his/her email address.

12. Our + singular noun Bienvenue dans ___ appartement ! Welcome to our apartment!

13. Our + plural noun On adore ___ voisins ! We love our neighbours!

14. Your (formal/plural) + singular noun Madame, montrez ___ passeport, s'il vous plaît. Madam, show your passport, please.

15. Your (formal/plural) + plural noun Vous avez ___ billets pour le concert ? Do you have your tickets for the concert?

16. Their + singular noun Les étudiants écoutent ___ professeur. The students listen to their teacher.

17. Their + plural noun Ils rangent ___ livres dans le sac. They put their books in the bag.

18. Ownership: est à + stressed pronoun Ce stylo, c'est ___ ! This pen, it's mine!

19. Ownership: est à + her Cette écharpe, c'est ___ . This scarf, it's hers.

20. Ownership: sont à + them (masc.) Ces vélos, ils sont ___ . These bikes, they're theirs.

22. Ownership: est à + you (informal) Ce sac sous la chaise, c'est ___ ? This bag under the chair, is it yours?

23. Ownership: est à + him Ce manteau, c'est ___ . This coat, it's his.

24. Ownership: sont à + them (fem.) Ces places, elles sont ___ . These seats, they're theirs. [fem.]

25. Ownership: est à + us Cet appartement, il est ___ . This apartment, it's ours.

26. Ownership: est à + you (formal) Ces documents, ils sont ___ , Monsieur ? These documents, are they yours, Sir?

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

Focus
Épisode 2 — Une famille au-dessus de la boulangerie
Focus

Focus
One correct answer per question unless a page explicitly says otherwise.
Unit 2 note
Unit 2 note: du is a contraction such as de + le, not a partitive article.

Reader alignment

Characters: Wei, Amara, Mateo, Yuki, Madame Benali, Madame Sow.
Settings: boulangerie, apartment above it, classroom, La Fougère, Paris streets, market, dinner, and Bilan.
LAF1201

Unit 3 · Paris, weather, and directions

Épisode 3 — Je suis perdue

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4

Weather, streets, métro routes, questions, attractions, and directions to find your way through Paris.

1Weather2City prepositions3Ordinals4Venir and prendre5Questions7Attractions8Directions9Place prepositions10Pronoun y★Paris and directions capstone
LAF1201

Unit 3 · Paris, weather, and directions

Épisode 3 — Je suis perdue

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
WeatherCity prepositionsOrdinalsVenir and prendreQuestionsAttractionsDirectionsPlace prepositionsPronoun yParis and directions capstone

Unit 3Weather

After the rain, someone is lost and the group must ask questions, describe the city, and give practical directions.
Paris navigation: weather expressions, prepositions of place (dans, sur, à côté de…), asking questions, ordinals (premier, deuxième…), and the pronoun y.
Focus — Describe the weather with the il-impersonal patterns: il fait beau, il pleut, il neige, il y a du vent. Tests the three weather verb families — faire for general weather, dedicated verbs (pleuvoir, neiger), and il y a for atmospheric phenomena.

1. A sunny morning in Paris Il ___ beau aujourd'hui. The weather is nice today.

2. Checking the forecast before packing En juillet à Paris, il fait très ___ . In July in Paris, it is very hot.

3. Winter morning Il fait ___ ce matin — mets un manteau ! It's cold this morning — put on a coat!

4. Looking out the window — it's grim Il fait ___ — je reste à la maison. The weather is bad — I'm staying home.

5. Autumn afternoon — pleasantly cool Il fait ___ mais c'est agréable. It's cool but pleasant.

6. Deciding whether to bring an umbrella Il y a ___ vent dehors. It's windy outside.

7. A perfect summer day Il y a ___ et il fait chaud. It's sunny and hot.

8. Thunderstorm warning Il y a ___ orage prévu ce soir. There's a storm forecast tonight.

9. Overcast sky Il y a beaucoup ___ nuages aujourd'hui. There are a lot of clouds today.

10. Caught without an umbrella Il ___ depuis ce matin. It has been raining since this morning.

11. Looking out the window in January Il ___ beaucoup cette semaine. It's snowing a lot this week.

12. Asking about the weather — ___ à Lyon en ce moment ? What's the weather like in Lyon right now?

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

Focus
Épisode 3 — Je suis perdue
Focus
Paris navigation: weather expressions, prepositions of place (dans, sur, à côté de…), asking questions, ordinals (premier, deuxième…), and the pronoun y.
Focus
One correct answer per question unless a page explicitly says otherwise.

Reader alignment

Characters: Wei, Amara, Mateo, Yuki, Madame Benali, Madame Sow.
Settings: boulangerie, apartment above it, classroom, La Fougère, Paris streets, market, dinner, and Bilan.
LAF1201

Unit 3 · Paris, weather, and directions

Épisode 3 — Je suis perdue

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
WeatherCity prepositionsOrdinalsVenir and prendreQuestionsAttractionsDirectionsPlace prepositionsPronoun yParis and directions capstone

Unit 3City prepositions

After the rain, someone is lost and the group must ask questions, describe the city, and give practical directions.
Paris navigation: weather expressions, prepositions of place (dans, sur, à côté de…), asking questions, ordinals (premier, deuxième…), and the pronoun y.
Focus — Pick the right preposition for cities and countries: à for cities (à Paris, à Tokyo), en for feminine countries (en France), au/aux for masculine and plural (au Canada, aux États-Unis). Drills preposition ↔ place type.

1. Telling someone where you live J'habite ___ Paris. I live in Paris.

2. A student studying abroad Elle étudie ___ France cette année. She is studying in France this year.

3. A colleague on a business trip Il est en voyage ___ Japon. He is on a business trip in Japan.

4. Planning a road trip On va ___ États-Unis cet été. We're going to the United States this summer.

5. Telling where you are from Je viens ___ Singapour. I come from Singapore.

6. Meeting someone at a conference Tu viens ___ Oslo ? You're from Oslo?

7. Describing an imported product Ce vin vient ___ France. This wine comes from France.

8. A product label Ce saké vient ___ Japon. This sake comes from Japan.

9. A student's exchange program Il fait son stage ___ Afrique. He is doing his internship in Africa.

10. Booking a hotel On a réservé un hôtel ___ Mexique. We booked a hotel in Mexico.

11. The flight destination board Le vol pour Londres part ___ 14h. The flight to London departs at 2 pm.

12. Asking where someone is from Elle vient ___ États-Unis. She comes from the United States.

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

Focus
Épisode 3 — Je suis perdue
Focus
Paris navigation: weather expressions, prepositions of place (dans, sur, à côté de…), asking questions, ordinals (premier, deuxième…), and the pronoun y.
Focus
One correct answer per question unless a page explicitly says otherwise.

Reader alignment

Characters: Wei, Amara, Mateo, Yuki, Madame Benali, Madame Sow.
Settings: boulangerie, apartment above it, classroom, La Fougère, Paris streets, market, dinner, and Bilan.
LAF1201

Unit 3 · Paris, weather, and directions

Épisode 3 — Je suis perdue

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
WeatherCity prepositionsOrdinalsVenir and prendreQuestionsAttractionsDirectionsPlace prepositionsPronoun yParis and directions capstone

Unit 3Ordinals

After the rain, someone is lost and the group must ask questions, describe the city, and give practical directions.
Paris navigation: weather expressions, prepositions of place (dans, sur, à côté de…), asking questions, ordinals (premier, deuxième…), and the pronoun y.
Focus — Form ordinal numbers — premier, deuxième, troisième… — for floors, dates, and rankings. Drills the premier/première irregularity (it's the only ordinal with a feminine form change) and the regular -ième suffix that handles everything else.

1. Hotel check-in Votre chambre est au ___ étage. Your room is on the first floor.

2. Asking for directions inside a building C'est la ___ porte à droite. It's the first door on the right.

3. Paris arrondissement Le Marais est dans le ___ arrondissement. Le Marais is in the third arrondissement.

4. Giving turn-by-turn directions Prenez la ___ rue à gauche. Take the third street on the left.

5. Naming a floor Le bureau est au ___ étage. The office is on the fourth floor.

6. Ranking a competition result Elle est arrivée ___ au marathon. She finished fifth in the marathon.

7. School grade / year Mon fils est en ___ (Year 7 equivalent). My son is in Year 7 (sixth grade).

8. A centenary celebration C'est le ___ anniversaire de la Tour Eiffel. It's the hundredth anniversary of the Eiffel Tower.

9. Ordering a round of drinks C'est notre ___ soirée ensemble cette année. It's our ninth evening together this year.

10. A race commentary Il passe en ___ position après le premier virage. He moves into tenth position after the first bend.

11. A book chapter Nous sommes au ___ chapitre. We are at the twentieth chapter.

12. The Eiffel Tower's floor Le restaurant se trouve au ___ étage de la Tour Eiffel. The restaurant is on the second floor of the Eiffel Tower.

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

Focus
Épisode 3 — Je suis perdue
Focus
Paris navigation: weather expressions, prepositions of place (dans, sur, à côté de…), asking questions, ordinals (premier, deuxième…), and the pronoun y.
Focus
One correct answer per question unless a page explicitly says otherwise.

Reader alignment

Characters: Wei, Amara, Mateo, Yuki, Madame Benali, Madame Sow.
Settings: boulangerie, apartment above it, classroom, La Fougère, Paris streets, market, dinner, and Bilan.
LAF1201

Unit 3 · Paris, weather, and directions

Épisode 3 — Je suis perdue

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
WeatherCity prepositionsOrdinalsVenir and prendreQuestionsAttractionsDirectionsPlace prepositionsPronoun yParis and directions capstone

Unit 3Venir and prendre

After the rain, someone is lost and the group must ask questions, describe the city, and give practical directions.
Paris navigation: weather expressions, prepositions of place (dans, sur, à côté de…), asking questions, ordinals (premier, deuxième…), and the pronoun y.
Focus — Conjugate two more high-traffic irregular verbs in parallel: venir (to come, with origin via de) and prendre (to take, including for transport — je prends le métro). Both have stem changes across the paradigm — different patterns, drilled together to keep them apart.

1. Asking where someone is from Je ___ de Singapour. I come from Singapore.

2. Confirming a friend's arrival Marie ___ nous rejoindre ce soir. Marie is coming to join us tonight.

3. Planning a group outing Nous ___ en métro. We're coming by metro.

4. The whole group is on its way Mes parents ___ nous rendre visite demain. My parents are coming to visit us tomorrow.

5. Just finished lunch On ___ manger. We just ate.

6. Taking the metro Je ___ la ligne 4 jusqu'à Montparnasse. I take line 4 to Montparnasse.

7. Asking a friend how they travel Tu ___ le bus ou le vélo ? Do you take the bus or the bike?

8. Ordering at a café — conjugate prendre (present) Moi, je ___ un café et un croissant. I'm having a coffee and a croissant.

9. Giving travel advice Pour aller à l'Opéra, vous ___ la ligne 3. To get to the Opéra, you take line 3.

10. Watching the tourists Les touristes ___ beaucoup de photos. The tourists take a lot of photos.

11. Deciding which route to take On ___ le RER B pour aller à l'aéroport. We take the RER B to get to the airport.

12. Phoning ahead to say you're on the way Vous ___ quand exactement ? When exactly are you coming?

13. Prendre — nous Nous ___ le métro tous les jours. We take the metro every day.

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

Épisode 3 — Je suis perdue

Focus

LAF1201

Unit 3 · Paris, weather, and directions

Épisode 3 — Je suis perdue

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
WeatherCity prepositionsOrdinalsVenir and prendreQuestionsAttractionsDirectionsPlace prepositionsPronoun yParis and directions capstone

Unit 3Questions

After the rain, someone is lost and the group must ask questions, describe the city, and give practical directions.
Paris navigation: weather expressions, prepositions of place (dans, sur, à côté de…), asking questions, ordinals (premier, deuxième…), and the pronoun y.
Focus — Form yes/no and information questions across the three registers: intonation (Tu viens ?), est-ce que (Est-ce que tu viens ?), and inversion (Viens-tu ?). Tests register fit — which form belongs in casual speech, which in writing or formal speech.

1. Asking a friend's destination — casual spoken Complete the casual question: ___ Where are you going?

2. The same question — neutral written style Complete the est-ce que question: ___ Where are you going?

3. Same question — formal / written register Complete the formal question: ___ Where are you going?

4. Asking about the name of a place Complete the question: ___ What is the name of this museum?

5. Asking about opening hours Complete the question: ___ What is the opening time?

6. Asking how someone travels Complete the question: ___ How are you coming?

7. Asking the price Complete the question: ___ How much is it?

8. Asking why someone is late Complete the question: ___ Why are you late?

9. Asking when the train leaves Complete the formal question: ___ When does the train leave?

10. Asking who is speaking Complete the question: ___ Who is it?

11. Asking where the Louvre is Complete the question: ___ Where is the Louvre?

12. Verb-vowel clash — the euphonic -t- Complete the formal question: ___ Is he arriving tonight?

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

LAF1201

Unit 3 · Paris, weather, and directions

Épisode 3 — Je suis perdue

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
WeatherCity prepositionsOrdinalsVenir and prendreQuestionsAttractionsDirectionsPlace prepositionsPronoun yParis and directions capstone

Unit 3Adjective agreement

After the rain, someone is lost and the group must ask questions, describe the city, and give practical directions.
Paris navigation: weather expressions, prepositions of place (dans, sur, à côté de…), asking questions, ordinals (premier, deuxième…), and the pronoun y.
Focus — Apply gender + number agreement across a wider range of adjectives than 0-08 introduced — including the irregular four-form set (beau/bel/belle/beaux/belles, nouveau/nouvel/nouvelle, vieux/vieil/vieille) used before vowel-initial masculine nouns.

1. Describing a museum C'est un musée ___ . It's an interesting museum.

2. Describing a cathedral C'est une cathédrale ___ . It's an old cathedral.

3. Describing the avenues Les avenues haussmanniennes sont très ___ . The Haussmann-era avenues are very wide.

4. Praising the food La baguette est vraiment ___ . The baguette is really good.

5. Admiring a façade C'est une ___ façade. It's a beautiful façade.

6. Describing a new neighbourhood C'est une ___ ligne de métro. It's a new metro line.

7. Describing the old quarter Ils habitent dans une ___ maison. They live in an old house.

8. Describing jacket colours Elle porte une veste ___ . She's wearing a brown jacket.

9. Recommending a restaurant C'est un ___ restaurant. It's a good restaurant.

10. A building description C'est une ___ avenue. It's a wide avenue.

11. Describing nationality (position after noun) C'est une ville ___ . It's a French city.

12. Two things worth seeing Le Louvre et Orsay sont deux musées ___ . The Louvre and Orsay are two famous museums.

13. Irregular masc. plural: beau Paris a de ___ jardins. Paris has beautiful gardens.

14. Irregular masc. plural: nouveau Il y a des ___ restaurants dans le quartier. There are new restaurants in the neighbourhood.

15. Irregular masc. plural: vieux Le quartier a de ___ bâtiments. The neighbourhood has old buildings.

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

LAF1201

Unit 3 · Paris, weather, and directions

Épisode 3 — Je suis perdue

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
WeatherCity prepositionsOrdinalsVenir and prendreQuestionsAttractionsDirectionsPlace prepositionsPronoun yParis and directions capstone

Unit 3Attractions

After the rain, someone is lost and the group must ask questions, describe the city, and give practical directions.
Paris navigation: weather expressions, prepositions of place (dans, sur, à côté de…), asking questions, ordinals (premier, deuxième…), and the pronoun y.
Focus — Talk about Paris landmarks — la tour Eiffel, le Louvre, Notre-Dame — with the right article and the right preposition for direction (à) or location (au, à la, à l'). Reapplies 2-02's contractions in a tourism context.

1. Planning a first day in Paris On va voir ___ ce soir. We're going to see the Eiffel Tower tonight.

2. Suggesting a museum visit Tu veux ___ le Louvre demain ? Do you want to visit the Louvre tomorrow?

3. Asking how to get there Comment est-ce qu'on va ___ ? How do we get to the Louvre?

4. Talking about a famous artwork Les impressionnistes sont exposés au ___ . The Impressionists are exhibited at the musée Orsay.

5. Describing a cathedral on the Île de la Cité On peut voir ___ depuis le Pont Neuf. You can see Notre-Dame from the Pont Neuf.

6. Taking the metro to Montmartre Le Sacré-Cœur se trouve ___ . The Sacré-Cœur is in Montmartre.

7. Walking down the famous avenue Nous nous promenons sur ___ . We're strolling along the Champs-Élysées.

8. Spotting the landmark from afar On voit ___ au bout des Champs-Élysées. You can see the Arc de Triomphe at the end of the Champs-Élysées.

9. Finding contemporary art L'art moderne est exposé au ___ . Modern art is exhibited at the Centre Pompidou.

10. Visiting the medieval chapel Les vitraux de ___ sont magnifiques. The stained-glass windows of the Sainte-Chapelle are magnificent.

11. A view from the Seine Le musée d'Orsay ___ sur la rive gauche. The Musée d'Orsay is located on the left bank.

12. A resident giving advice Je vais ___ ma famille ce week-end. I'm going to visit my family this weekend.

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

LAF1201

Unit 3 · Paris, weather, and directions

Épisode 3 — Je suis perdue

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
WeatherCity prepositionsOrdinalsVenir and prendreQuestionsAttractionsDirectionsPlace prepositionsPronoun yParis and directions capstone

Unit 3Directions

After the rain, someone is lost and the group must ask questions, describe the city, and give practical directions.
Paris navigation: weather expressions, prepositions of place (dans, sur, à côté de…), asking questions, ordinals (premier, deuxième…), and the pronoun y.
Focus — Give and follow directions: tournez à droite, allez tout droit, prenez la deuxième rue. Drills the vous-imperative (the politeness default for strangers) and the spatial vocabulary of orientation — left, right, straight, around.

1. Asking a stranger for help ___ , pour aller à la gare, s'il vous plaît ? Excuse me, how do I get to the station, please?

2. Directing someone at a junction Au feu rouge, ___ . At the red light, turn left.

3. Keeping straight at the roundabout Au rond-point, ___ jusqu'à la pharmacie. At the roundabout, continue straight on until the pharmacy.

4. Getting across to the other side ___ et vous verrez la cathédrale à gauche. Cross the bridge and you'll see the cathedral on your left.

5. Specifying which street to take ___ Victor Hugo jusqu'au bout. Take rue Victor Hugo all the way to the end.

6. Counting turns from the corner Prenez ___ après le café. Take the first street on the right after the café.

7. How far is the hotel? L'hôtel est ___ d'ici. The hotel is ten minutes on foot from here.

8. Encouraging a lost tourist Ne vous inquiétez pas — ___ du tout ! Don't worry — you're not far at all!

9. Finding the exact spot La boulangerie est ___ la rue de Rivoli et de la rue du Louvre. The bakery is at the corner of rue de Rivoli and rue du Louvre.

10. Walking to a landmark ___ la place de la République, puis tournez à gauche. Go as far as Place de la République, then turn left.

11. Describing a landmark's position La mairie se trouve ___ la gare. The town hall is opposite the station.

12. Telling someone they've arrived Continuez encore un peu et ___ ! Keep going a little further and you're there!

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

LAF1201

Unit 3 · Paris, weather, and directions

Épisode 3 — Je suis perdue

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
WeatherCity prepositionsOrdinalsVenir and prendreQuestionsAttractionsDirectionsPlace prepositionsPronoun yParis and directions capstone

Unit 3Place prepositions

After the rain, someone is lost and the group must ask questions, describe the city, and give practical directions.
Paris navigation: weather expressions, prepositions of place (dans, sur, à côté de…), asking questions, ordinals (premier, deuxième…), and the pronoun y.
Focus — Pick the right preposition for relative location: dans, sur, sous, devant, derrière, à côté de, en face de, entre. Tests both the spatial relationship meaning and the de-contraction on the compound prepositions (à côté du musée, en face des magasins).

1. Looking for something misplaced Mon passeport est ___ le sac. My passport is in the bag.

2. Setting the table Les assiettes sont ___ la table. The plates are on the table.

3. A cat's favourite hiding spot Le chat dort ___ le lit. The cat is sleeping under the bed.

4. Seating arrangement at a restaurant Marie est assise ___ Paul et Lucie. Marie is sitting between Paul and Lucie.

5. Waiting outside the cinema On se retrouve ___ le cinéma à 19h. We're meeting in front of the cinema at 7 pm.

6. Describing the garden layout Il y a un jardin ___ la maison. There is a garden behind the house.

7. Finding the pharmacy La pharmacie est ___ la boulangerie. The pharmacy is next to the bakery.

8. Locating the hotel across the square L'hôtel est ___ parc. The hotel is opposite the park.

9. Choosing a café with a view Je préfère un café ___ la Tour Eiffel. I prefer a café near the Eiffel Tower.

10. The airport is not in the city centre L'aéroport est assez ___ centre-ville. The airport is quite far from the city centre.

11. The flat is on an upper floor L'appartement est ___ le restaurant. The flat is above the restaurant.

12. The temperature drops below freezing La température est tombée ___ zéro. The temperature dropped below zero.

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

Focus
Épisode 3 — Je suis perdue
Focus

Focus

Settings: boulangerie, apartment above it, classroom, La Fougère, Paris streets, market, dinner, and Bilan.
LAF1201

Unit 3 · Paris, weather, and directions

Épisode 3 — Je suis perdue

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
WeatherCity prepositionsOrdinalsVenir and prendreQuestionsAttractionsDirectionsPlace prepositionsPronoun yParis and directions capstone

Unit 3Pronoun y

After the rain, someone is lost and the group must ask questions, describe the city, and give practical directions.
Paris navigation: weather expressions, prepositions of place (dans, sur, à côté de…), asking questions, ordinals (premier, deuxième…), and the pronoun y.
Focus — Replace a place expression (à + place) with the pronoun y and put it in the right slot. Four variables drilled at once: with or without a modal (j'y vais vs on va y aller), with or without negation (j'y vais vs je n'y vais pas), before a vowel or a consonant (the je → j' elision still fires before y), and the fixed expressions (vas-y, on y est, il y a).

1. Already been to that museum — Tu vas au Louvre ? — Non, ___ suis déjà allé. — Are you going to the Louvre? — No, I've already been there.

2. Planning the weekend On va à la plage ? — Oui, on ___ va demain. Are we going to the beach? — Yes, we're going there tomorrow.

3. Hesitating about Paris Je ___ vais pas encore cette année. I'm not going there yet this year.

4. Asking if someone knows the place Tu vas souvent à ce restaurant ? Tu ___ vas parfois ? Have you already been there, to that restaurant?

5. Modal verb + infinitive On va ___ aller la semaine prochaine. We are going to go there next week.

6. Urging someone to go Tu hésites encore ? ___ ! Still hesitating? Go on / Go there!

7. Telling someone not to go C'est dangereux — ___ ! It's dangerous — don't go there!

8. Confirming the meeting point On approche — on ___ arrive ! We've arrived — we're there!

9. Talking about a neighbourhood Tu connais le Marais ? — Oui, ___ habite. Do you know the Marais? — Yes, I live there.

10. A favourite café Ce café ? J' ___ vais souvent le matin. This café? I often go there in the morning.

11. Things to do at the market Au marché, ___ beaucoup de légumes frais. At the market, there are lots of fresh vegetables.

12. Asking about things at the hotel ___ une piscine dans cet hôtel ? Is there a swimming pool in this hotel?

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

Focus
Épisode 3 — Je suis perdue
Focus

Focus
One correct answer per question unless a page explicitly says otherwise.

Reader alignment

Characters: Wei, Amara, Mateo, Yuki, Madame Benali, Madame Sow.
Settings: boulangerie, apartment above it, classroom, La Fougère, Paris streets, market, dinner, and Bilan.
LAF1201

Unit 3 · Paris, weather, and directions

Épisode 3 — Je suis perdue

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
WeatherCity prepositionsOrdinalsVenir and prendreQuestionsAttractionsDirectionsPlace prepositionsPronoun yParis and directions capstone

Unit 3Paris and directions capstone

After the rain, someone is lost and the group must ask questions, describe the city, and give practical directions.
Paris navigation: weather expressions, prepositions of place (dans, sur, à côté de…), asking questions, ordinals (premier, deuxième…), and the pronoun y.
Focus — One day-trip to Paris, told in twelve frames. Morning weather at the window, the journey to Paris, the hotel floor, the just-arrived moment at the Eiffel Tower, asking a local when the museum opens, admiring a Haussmann building, taking the metro to the Louvre, locating La Joconde, finding the exit, the café between the bookshop and bakery, recommending it, and a closing line that uses y to look back on it all. The arc — leaving home → seeing the city → coming back to talk about it — is what carries you through weather, prepositions, ordinals, questions, place words, and finally the pronoun y in the order they'd actually surface on the trip.

1. Morning in Paris — checking the weather Regarde par la fenêtre — ___ aujourd'hui ! Look out the window — the weather is nice today!

2. Planning the trip from home Nous allons ___ Paris pour le week-end. We're going to Paris for the weekend.

3. Finding the hotel room Notre chambre est au ___ étage. Our room is on the fifth floor.

4. Just arrived at the Eiffel Tower On ___ arriver à la Tour Eiffel. We've just arrived at the Eiffel Tower.

5. Asking a local for information ___ est-ce que le musée ouvre ? What time does the museum open?

6. Describing a Haussmann building C'est un ___ bâtiment haussmannien. It's a beautiful Haussmann building.

7. Getting the metro to the museum Pour aller au Louvre, on ___ le métro ligne 1. To get to the Louvre, you take Metro line 1.

8. Inside the Louvre — locating a painting La Joconde ___ dans la salle des États. The Mona Lisa is located in the Salle des États.

9. Leaving the Louvre — asking for the exit Pour sortir, continuez ___ jusqu'à la pyramide. To exit, continue straight ahead to the pyramid.

10. Finding the café for lunch Le café est ___ la librairie et la boulangerie. The café is between the bookshop and the bakery.

11. Recommending the café to a friend Tu connais ce café ? Tu vas ___ aller — c'est excellent ! Do you know this café? You are going to go there — it's excellent!

12. End of the day — looking back On ___ est souvent aujourd'hui ! We are there a lot today!

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

Focus
Épisode 3 — Je suis perdue
Focus
Paris navigation: weather expressions, prepositions of place (dans, sur, à côté de…), asking questions, ordinals (premier, deuxième…), and the pronoun y.
Focus

Reader alignment

LAF1201

Unit 4 · Le grand dîner and Bilan

Épisode 4 — Le grand dîner; Épisode 5 — Bilan

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4

Food vocabulary, partitives, shopping, cooking, giving advice, and reflecting after le grand dîner.

1Food vocabulary2Partitive articles3Partitives in the negative4Manger and boire5Frequency6Demonstratives7Futur proche8Shopping9Advice10Restaurant language★Dinner and Bilan capstone
LAF1201

Unit 4 · Le grand dîner and Bilan

Épisode 4 — Le grand dîner; Épisode 5 — Bilan

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
Food vocabularyPartitive articlesPartitives in the negativeManger and boireFrequencyDemonstrativesFutur procheShoppingAdviceRestaurant languageDinner and Bilan capstone

Unit 4Food vocabulary

The group prepares a real dinner for Madame Benali, neighbours, and the little family above the boulangerie, then closes with a Bilan.
Le grand dîner: food vocabulary, partitives (du, de la, des) for unspecified quantity, shopping verbs, frequency words, demonstratives (ce, cette, ces), and the futur proche.
Focus — Recognise and produce the core food lexicon — meals, ingredients, courses, drinks. Sets up the next two exercises: every food word here will need a partitive (du, de la, des, de l') when used as an unspecified quantity in 4-02.

1. Morning routine Je prends ___ à sept heures. I have breakfast at seven o'clock.

2. Ordering at a restaurant Je prends ___ rôti avec des légumes. I'll have the roast chicken with vegetables.

3. Describing a balanced plate Il mange ___ et des tomates. He eats salad and tomatoes.

4. Talking about a staple food Les Français mangent beaucoup de ___ . The French eat a lot of bread.

5. A healthy snack Pour le goûter, elle mange ___ . For her afternoon snack, she eats an apple.

6. Staying hydrated Je bois ___ minérale. I drink mineral water.

7. Sunday lunch conversation On mange ___ bœuf bourguignon le dimanche. We eat beef bourguignon on Sundays.

8. Vegetarian choice Elle préfère manger ___ plutôt que de la viande. She prefers to eat vegetables rather than meat.

9. Breakfast eggs Je mange deux ___ le matin. I eat two eggs in the morning.

10. Cheese course En France, on mange ___ après le plat principal. In France, people eat cheese after the main course.

11. Dinner drink Elle boit ___ rouge au dîner. She drinks red wine at dinner.

12. Pasta night On va manger ___ ce soir. We're going to eat pasta tonight.

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

Focus
Épisode 4 — Le grand dîner; Épisode 5 — Bilan
Focus
Le grand dîner: food vocabulary, partitives (du, de la, des) for unspecified quantity, shopping verbs, frequency words, demonstratives (ce, cette, ces), and the futur proche.
Focus
One correct answer per question unless a page explicitly says otherwise.
Unit 4 note
Unit 4 note: this is the first explicit partitive unit for food and quantities.

Reader alignment

Characters: Wei, Amara, Mateo, Yuki, Madame Benali, Madame Sow.
Settings: boulangerie, apartment above it, classroom, La Fougère, Paris streets, market, dinner, and Bilan.
LAF1201

Unit 4 · Le grand dîner and Bilan

Épisode 4 — Le grand dîner; Épisode 5 — Bilan

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
Food vocabularyPartitive articlesPartitives in the negativeManger and boireFrequencyDemonstrativesFutur procheShoppingAdviceRestaurant languageDinner and Bilan capstone

Unit 4Partitive articles

The group prepares a real dinner for Madame Benali, neighbours, and the little family above the boulangerie, then closes with a Bilan.
Le grand dîner: food vocabulary, partitives (du, de la, des) for unspecified quantity, shopping verbs, frequency words, demonstratives (ce, cette, ces), and the futur proche.
Focus — Pick the right partitive — du, de la, de l', des — for unspecified quantities of food: je mange du pain, je bois de l'eau, je prends des pommes. The contrast drilled hardest: partitive after eating/drinking verbs vs definite article after liking verbs (from 2-07). Partitives are unbounded — du pain means some unspecified quantity of bread. Definites are bounded — le pain means bread as a whole category. Eating takes a portion (unbounded); liking takes the concept (bounded).

1. Morning coffee Chaque matin, je bois ___ café avant de partir. Every morning, I drink coffee before leaving.

2. Cooking dinner On prépare ___ poulet avec des herbes. We're preparing some chicken with herbs.

3. Lunch salad Elle mange ___ salade tous les midis. She eats salad every lunchtime.

4. Drinking water Tu veux ___ eau ? Do you want some water?

5. Fruit bowl Il y a ___ oranges sur la table. There are some oranges on the table.

6. General food preference J'adore ___ pizza. I love pizza.

7. Sports habit Le samedi, il fait ___ sport. On Saturdays, he does sport.

8. Buying cheese at the market Je voudrais ___ fromage, s'il vous plaît. I'd like some cheese, please.

9. Recipe instruction Ajoutez ___ huile d'olive. Add some olive oil.

10. Baking a cake La recette demande ___ sucre et de la farine. The recipe calls for sugar and flour.

11. Vegetable soup Dans la soupe, il y a ___ carottes et du céleri. In the soup there are some carrots and some celery.

12. Habitual preference vs current action J'aime la bière, mais ce soir je bois ___ vin. I like beer, but tonight I'm drinking some wine.

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

Focus
Épisode 4 — Le grand dîner; Épisode 5 — Bilan
Focus
Le grand dîner: food vocabulary, partitives (du, de la, des) for unspecified quantity, shopping verbs, frequency words, demonstratives (ce, cette, ces), and the futur proche.
Focus
One correct answer per question unless a page explicitly says otherwise.
Unit 4 note
Unit 4 note: this is the first explicit partitive unit for food and quantities.

Reader alignment

Characters: Wei, Amara, Mateo, Yuki, Madame Benali, Madame Sow.
Settings: boulangerie, apartment above it, classroom, La Fougère, Paris streets, market, dinner, and Bilan.
LAF1201

Unit 4 · Le grand dîner and Bilan

Épisode 4 — Le grand dîner; Épisode 5 — Bilan

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
Food vocabularyPartitive articlesPartitives in the negativeManger and boireFrequencyDemonstrativesFutur procheShoppingAdviceRestaurant languageDinner and Bilan capstone

Unit 4Partitives in the negative

The group prepares a real dinner for Madame Benali, neighbours, and the little family above the boulangerie, then closes with a Bilan.
Le grand dîner: food vocabulary, partitives (du, de la, des) for unspecified quantity, shopping verbs, frequency words, demonstratives (ce, cette, ces), and the futur proche.
Focus — Apply the partitive-collapse-to-de rule under negation: je mange du pain → je ne mange pas DE pain, j'ai des amis → je n'ai pas D'amis. The single most counter-intuitive rule for English speakers, drilled in isolation. Under negation, the unbounded quantity collapses to zero: du pain → pas de pain. No portion at all — the de alone signals "none of."

1. Vegetarian diet Elle ne mange pas ___ viande. She doesn't eat meat.

2. No more bread Je ne mange plus ___ pain — je fais un régime. I no longer eat bread — I'm on a diet.

3. Can't stand water Il ne boit pas ___ eau — seulement du jus. He doesn't drink water — only juice.

4. Generous portion Elle mange ___ légumes chaque jour. She eats a lot of vegetables every day.

5. Too much sugar Ce gâteau contient ___ sucre. This cake contains too much sugar.

6. Not enough salt Il n'y a pas ___ sel dans cette soupe. There isn't enough salt in this soup.

7. Buying olive oil Je prends ___ huile d'olive. I'll take a bottle of olive oil.

8. Recipe: pasta dough La recette demande ___ farine. The recipe calls for 200 g of flour.

9. Exception: c'est + partitive — C'est du café ? — Non, ___ café — c'est du thé. — Is it coffee? — No, it's not coffee — it's tea.

10. Small amount of milk Tu veux ___ lait dans ton café ? Do you want a little milk in your coffee?

11. No vegetables left Il n'y a plus ___ légumes au marché. There are no more vegetables at the market.

12. Lots of fruit Il mange ___ abricots en été. He eats a lot of apricots in summer.

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

Focus
Épisode 4 — Le grand dîner; Épisode 5 — Bilan
Focus
Le grand dîner: food vocabulary, partitives (du, de la, des) for unspecified quantity, shopping verbs, frequency words, demonstratives (ce, cette, ces), and the futur proche.
Focus

Unit 4 note
Unit 4 note: this is the first explicit partitive unit for food and quantities.

Reader alignment

Settings: boulangerie, apartment above it, classroom, La Fougère, Paris streets, market, dinner, and Bilan.
LAF1201

Unit 4 · Le grand dîner and Bilan

Épisode 4 — Le grand dîner; Épisode 5 — Bilan

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
Food vocabularyPartitive articlesPartitives in the negativeManger and boireFrequencyDemonstrativesFutur procheShoppingAdviceRestaurant languageDinner and Bilan capstone

Unit 4Manger and boire

The group prepares a real dinner for Madame Benali, neighbours, and the little family above the boulangerie, then closes with a Bilan.
Le grand dîner: food vocabulary, partitives (du, de la, des) for unspecified quantity, shopping verbs, frequency words, demonstratives (ce, cette, ces), and the futur proche.
Focus — Conjugate the two eating/drinking verbs that anchor every food sentence. Manger is regular but with the nous mangeons spelling shift (e kept before -ons for soft g); boire is irregular (bois, bois, boit, buvons, buvez, boivent) — different problem, drilled side by side.

1. Your daily habit Le matin, je ___ des œufs. In the morning I eat eggs.

2. What does she eat? Elle ___ de la salade au déjeuner. She eats salad at lunch.

3. Cooking together Nous ___ toujours ensemble le soir. We always eat together in the evening.

4. The children's dinner Les enfants ___ des pâtes ce soir. The children are eating pasta tonight.

5. Your drink preference Je ___ de l'eau toute la journée. I drink water all day long.

6. Evening drink Il ___ du vin rouge avec le dîner. He drinks red wine with dinner.

7. Hydrating as a group Nous ___ beaucoup d'eau pendant l'entraînement. We drink a lot of water during training.

8. The guests' choice Les invités ___ du champagne. The guests are drinking champagne.

9. Lunch ordering Je ___ une omelette et je bois du café. I'm eating an omelette and I'm drinking coffee.

10. Diet habit Est-ce que vous ___ des légumes tous les jours ? Do you eat vegetables every day?

11. She doesn't drink coffee Elle ne ___ pas de café. She doesn't drink coffee.

12. Recipe: eat immediately On ___ ce plat chaud, tout de suite. You eat this dish hot, straight away.

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

Focus
Épisode 4 — Le grand dîner; Épisode 5 — Bilan
Focus
Le grand dîner: food vocabulary, partitives (du, de la, des) for unspecified quantity, shopping verbs, frequency words, demonstratives (ce, cette, ces), and the futur proche.
Focus
One correct answer per question unless a page explicitly says otherwise.
Unit 4 note
Unit 4 note: this is the first explicit partitive unit for food and quantities.

Reader alignment

Characters: Wei, Amara, Mateo, Yuki, Madame Benali, Madame Sow.
Settings: boulangerie, apartment above it, classroom, La Fougère, Paris streets, market, dinner, and Bilan.
LAF1201

Unit 4 · Le grand dîner and Bilan

Épisode 4 — Le grand dîner; Épisode 5 — Bilan

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
Food vocabularyPartitive articlesPartitives in the negativeManger and boireFrequencyDemonstrativesFutur procheShoppingAdviceRestaurant languageDinner and Bilan capstone

Unit 4Frequency

The group prepares a real dinner for Madame Benali, neighbours, and the little family above the boulangerie, then closes with a Bilan.
Le grand dîner: food vocabulary, partitives (du, de la, des) for unspecified quantity, shopping verbs, frequency words, demonstratives (ce, cette, ces), and the futur proche.
Focus — Place frequency adverbs — toujours, souvent, parfois, rarement, jamais — in the right slot. Drills the post-verb position (je mange souvent…, not je souvent mange…) and the ne … jamais two-piece negative.

1. Describing a perfect habit Je mange ___ du petit-déjeuner avant de partir. I always eat breakfast before leaving.

2. Regular lunch habit Elle mange ___ de la salade au déjeuner. She often eats salad at lunch.

3. Occasional treat Nous mangeons ___ du gâteau le week-end. We sometimes eat cake at the weekend.

4. A rare indulgence Je mange ___ de la restauration rapide. I rarely eat fast food.

5. A strict diet Elle ne mange ___ de sucre. She never eats sugar.

6. Never drinks alcohol Il ne boit ___ d'alcool — pas une seule goutte en 20 ans. He never drinks alcohol — not a single drop in 20 years.

7. Adverb position check Tu manges ___ de la viande ? Do you often eat meat?

8. Every single day Je bois du café ___ . I drink coffee every day.

9. From time to time On mange au restaurant ___ . We eat at the restaurant from time to time.

10. Adverb placement Je mange ___ dehors la semaine. I often eat out during the week.

11. Survey response Je mange ___ du poisson — c'est mon plat préféré ! I often eat fish — it is my favourite dish!

12. Evaluating a habit Je ne mange ___ de légumes — c'est mauvais pour la santé. I never eat vegetables — it's bad for your health.

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

Focus
Épisode 4 — Le grand dîner; Épisode 5 — Bilan
Focus
Le grand dîner: food vocabulary, partitives (du, de la, des) for unspecified quantity, shopping verbs, frequency words, demonstratives (ce, cette, ces), and the futur proche.
Focus
One correct answer per question unless a page explicitly says otherwise.
Unit 4 note
Unit 4 note: this is the first explicit partitive unit for food and quantities.

Reader alignment

Characters: Wei, Amara, Mateo, Yuki, Madame Benali, Madame Sow.
Settings: boulangerie, apartment above it, classroom, La Fougère, Paris streets, market, dinner, and Bilan.
LAF1201

Unit 4 · Le grand dîner and Bilan

Épisode 4 — Le grand dîner; Épisode 5 — Bilan

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
Food vocabularyPartitive articlesPartitives in the negativeManger and boireFrequencyDemonstrativesFutur procheShoppingAdviceRestaurant languageDinner and Bilan capstone

Unit 4Demonstratives

The group prepares a real dinner for Madame Benali, neighbours, and the little family above the boulangerie, then closes with a Bilan.
Le grand dîner: food vocabulary, partitives (du, de la, des) for unspecified quantity, shopping verbs, frequency words, demonstratives (ce, cette, ces), and the futur proche.
Focus — Pick ce, cet, cette, ces for "this/these" with food and shopping items. Drills gender + number agreement and the cet trap — masculine singular only before vowel-initial nouns (cet ami, cet hôtel) — which trips up almost everyone the first time.

1. Pointing to a dish Je voudrais ___ plat, s'il vous plaît. I'd like this dish, please.

2. Planning the afternoon On fait les courses ___ après-midi. We're doing the shopping this afternoon.

3. Discussing this week's menu Qu'est-ce qu'on mange ___ semaine ? What are we eating this week?

4. These vegetables look fresh ___ légumes ont l'air très frais. These vegetables look very fresh.

5. This evening's plan On va au restaurant ___ soir. We're going to the restaurant this evening.

6. Recommending a restaurant Tu connais ___ endroit ? Do you know this place?

7. Commenting on a recipe J'adore ___ recette. I love this recipe.

8. These prices are high ___ prix sont trop élevés. These prices are too high.

9. This morning's breakfast Je mange des crêpes ___ matin. I eat crêpes this morning.

10. This cheese smells strong ___ fromage sent très fort ! This cheese smells very strong!

11. Choosing a summer fruit On mange beaucoup d'abricots ___ été. We eat a lot of apricots this summer.

12. This-vs-that distinction Je préfère ce gâteau ___ à ce gâteau-là. I prefer this cake (here) to that cake (there).

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

Focus
Épisode 4 — Le grand dîner; Épisode 5 — Bilan
Focus
Le grand dîner: food vocabulary, partitives (du, de la, des) for unspecified quantity, shopping verbs, frequency words, demonstratives (ce, cette, ces), and the futur proche.
Focus
One correct answer per question unless a page explicitly says otherwise.
Unit 4 note
Unit 4 note: this is the first explicit partitive unit for food and quantities.

Reader alignment

Characters: Wei, Amara, Mateo, Yuki, Madame Benali, Madame Sow.
Settings: boulangerie, apartment above it, classroom, La Fougère, Paris streets, market, dinner, and Bilan.
LAF1201

Unit 4 · Le grand dîner and Bilan

Épisode 4 — Le grand dîner; Épisode 5 — Bilan

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
Food vocabularyPartitive articlesPartitives in the negativeManger and boireFrequencyDemonstrativesFutur procheShoppingAdviceRestaurant languageDinner and Bilan capstone

Unit 4Futur proche

The group prepares a real dinner for Madame Benali, neighbours, and the little family above the boulangerie, then closes with a Bilan.
Le grand dîner: food vocabulary, partitives (du, de la, des) for unspecified quantity, shopping verbs, frequency words, demonstratives (ce, cette, ces), and the futur proche.
Focus — Build the near future with aller (conjugated) + infinitive: je vais manger, nous allons partir. Reuses 2-02's aller conjugation under a new role — aller as auxiliary, not as motion. The infinitive that follows never changes shape.

1. Tonight's dinner plan Je ___ manger de la pizza ce soir. I'm going to eat pizza this evening.

2. Offering a drink Tu ___ boire du café ? Are you going to drink some coffee?

3. She's cooking tonight Elle ___ préparer le dîner ce soir. She's going to prepare dinner this evening.

4. We have plans this week Nous ___ cuisiner ensemble cette semaine. We're going to cook together this week.

5. Taking your order Qu'est-ce que vous ___ commander ? What are you going to order?

6. Weekend market trip Ils ___ faire les courses demain. They're going to do the shopping tomorrow.

7. Skipping meat tonight Je ne vais pas ___ de viande ce soir. I'm not going to eat meat this evening.

8. No coffee at night (formal written context) Je [message écrit à ma famille d'accueil] ___ boire de café le soir. I'm not going to drink coffee in the evening.

9. Dinner plans this evening On va manger au restaurant ___ . We're going to eat at the restaurant this evening.

10. Baking a birthday cake Vous allez ___ un gâteau pour l'anniversaire ? Are you going to prepare a cake for the birthday?

11. Trying a new restaurant On ___ essayer ce nouveau restaurant. We're going to try this new restaurant.

12. He's never eating sushi Il ne va ___ manger de sushi. He's never going to eat sushi.

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

Focus
Épisode 4 — Le grand dîner; Épisode 5 — Bilan
Focus
Le grand dîner: food vocabulary, partitives (du, de la, des) for unspecified quantity, shopping verbs, frequency words, demonstratives (ce, cette, ces), and the futur proche.
Focus
One correct answer per question unless a page explicitly says otherwise.
Unit 4 note
