Reader alignment

Characters: Wei, Amara, Mateo, Yuki, Madame Benali, Madame Sow.
Settings: boulangerie, apartment above it, classroom, La Fougère, Paris streets, market, dinner, and Bilan.
LAF1201

Unit 4 · Le grand dîner and Bilan

Épisode 4 — Le grand dîner; Épisode 5 — Bilan

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
Food vocabularyPartitive articlesPartitives in the negativeManger and boireFrequencyDemonstrativesFutur procheShoppingAdviceRestaurant languageDinner and Bilan capstone

Unit 4Shopping

The group prepares a real dinner for Madame Benali, neighbours, and the little family above the boulangerie, then closes with a Bilan.
Le grand dîner: food vocabulary, partitives (du, de la, des) for unspecified quantity, shopping verbs, frequency words, demonstratives (ce, cette, ces), and the futur proche.
Focus — Run a shopping exchange: ask for quantities (un kilo de, une bouteille de, deux cents grammes de), prices, and totals. Drills expressions of quantity + de — note that de stays bare, no article: un kilo de pommes, never un kilo des pommes.

1. Where to buy bread Pour acheter une baguette, on va à la ___ . To buy a baguette, you go to the bakery.

2. Where to buy meat Pour acheter du poulet, on va à la ___ . To buy chicken, you go to the butcher's.

3. Where to buy staples Pour acheter du riz et des pâtes, on va à l' ___ . To buy rice and pasta, you go to the grocery store.

4. Finding camembert Je cherche du camembert. Je vais à la ___ . I'm looking for camembert. I'm going to the cheese shop.

5. Buying fresh salmon Pour acheter du saumon frais, on va à la ___ . To buy fresh salmon, you go to the fish shop.

6. At the bakery counter — polite request Bonjour, [au comptoir de la boulangerie] ___ deux croissants, s'il vous plaît. Hello, I'd like two croissants, please.

7. Asking the total Le client demande : « ___ ? » How much is it?

8. Shopkeeper asks if you need more Vendeur : « ___ ? » And with this? / Anything else?

9. Closing the purchase Vendeur : « Et avec ceci ? » — Client : « ___ , merci. » That's all, thank you.

10. Paying at the market Je peux payer ___ ? Can I pay by card?

11. Ordering at the deli Il me faut ___ jambon, s'il vous plaît. I need a slice of ham, please.

12. At the vegetable stall Je voudrais ___ tomates, s'il vous plaît. I'd like a kilo of tomatoes, please.

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

Focus
Épisode 4 — Le grand dîner; Épisode 5 — Bilan
Focus
Le grand dîner: food vocabulary, partitives (du, de la, des) for unspecified quantity, shopping verbs, frequency words, demonstratives (ce, cette, ces), and the futur proche.
Focus
One correct answer per question unless a page explicitly says otherwise.
Unit 4 note
Unit 4 note: this is the first explicit partitive unit for food and quantities.

Reader alignment

Characters: Wei, Amara, Mateo, Yuki, Madame Benali, Madame Sow.
Settings: boulangerie, apartment above it, classroom, La Fougère, Paris streets, market, dinner, and Bilan.
LAF1201

Unit 4 · Le grand dîner and Bilan

Épisode 4 — Le grand dîner; Épisode 5 — Bilan

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
Food vocabularyPartitive articlesPartitives in the negativeManger and boireFrequencyDemonstrativesFutur procheShoppingAdviceRestaurant languageDinner and Bilan capstone

Unit 4Advice

The group prepares a real dinner for Madame Benali, neighbours, and the little family above the boulangerie, then closes with a Bilan.
Le grand dîner: food vocabulary, partitives (du, de la, des) for unspecified quantity, shopping verbs, frequency words, demonstratives (ce, cette, ces), and the futur proche.
Focus — Give cooking and eating advice with the tu-imperative and il faut + infinitive. Drills the imperative formation (mange / mangez, prends / prenez, with the silent -s dropped on -er verbs in tu) and the impersonal il faut for general necessity.

1. Recipe instruction Pour faire une bonne omelette, ___ casser trois œufs. To make a good omelette, you need to break three eggs.

2. Booking in advance ___ réserver une table à l'avance dans ce restaurant. You need to book a table in advance at this restaurant.

3. What not to do in the kitchen Il ne ___ pas trop saler la soupe. You must not add too much salt to the soup.

4. General health advice Pour rester en bonne santé, ___ manger équilibré. To stay healthy, you need to eat a balanced diet.

5. Card payment is available Si tu veux payer par carte, ___ utiliser la machine là-bas. If you want to pay by card, you can use the machine over there.

6. The full menu is available ___ commander n'importe quel plat du menu — c'est inclus. You can order any dish on the menu — it's included.

7. Sharing is possible Tu n'as pas très faim ? ___ partager le plat avec moi. You're not very hungry? You can share the dish with me.

8. Vegetarian option available Si tu es végétarien, ___ choisir la quiche aux légumes. If you're vegetarian, you can choose the vegetable quiche.

9. Eating habits need improving Tu manges trop de fast food. ___ cuisiner plus souvent. You eat too much fast food. You should cook more often.

10. Restaurant recommendation ___ essayer le nouveau restaurant près du marché — il est excellent. You should try the new restaurant near the market — it's excellent.

11. Cheese tip — use the conditional Si tu aimes le fromage, ___ aller à la fromagerie rue Mouffetard. If you like cheese, you should go to the cheese shop on rue Mouffetard.

12. Tasting a new dish — use the conditional ___ goûter ce fromage — il est vraiment délicieux. You should taste this cheese — it's really delicious.

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

Focus
Épisode 4 — Le grand dîner; Épisode 5 — Bilan
Focus
Le grand dîner: food vocabulary, partitives (du, de la, des) for unspecified quantity, shopping verbs, frequency words, demonstratives (ce, cette, ces), and the futur proche.
Focus
One correct answer per question unless a page explicitly says otherwise.
Unit 4 note

Reader alignment

Settings: boulangerie, apartment above it, classroom, La Fougère, Paris streets, market, dinner, and Bilan.
LAF1201

Unit 4 · Le grand dîner and Bilan

Épisode 4 — Le grand dîner; Épisode 5 — Bilan

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
Food vocabularyPartitive articlesPartitives in the negativeManger and boireFrequencyDemonstrativesFutur procheShoppingAdviceRestaurant languageDinner and Bilan capstone

Unit 4Restaurant language

The group prepares a real dinner for Madame Benali, neighbours, and the little family above the boulangerie, then closes with a Bilan.
Le grand dîner: food vocabulary, partitives (du, de la, des) for unspecified quantity, shopping verbs, frequency words, demonstratives (ce, cette, ces), and the futur proche.
Focus — Run a full restaurant exchange — booking, ordering, asking, paying. Drills register: je voudrais (polite, expected) over je veux (blunt, avoid), plus the service-French formulas l'addition s'il vous plaît, c'est pour moi, on partage.

1. la carte vs le menu Excusez-moi, je peux voir ___ , s'il vous plaît ? Excuse me, may I see the menu, please?

2. Making a reservation Je voudrais ___ une table pour deux personnes ce soir. I'd like to book a table for two people this evening.

3. Waiter approaches the table Le serveur demande : « Vous désirez ___ ? » What would you like to have?

4. Ordering the main course Et ___ plat principal, je vais prendre le bœuf bourguignon. And for the main course, I'll have the beef bourguignon.

5. Course before the main La ___ vient avant le plat principal. The starter comes before the main course.

6. The food is wonderful Ce bœuf bourguignon est vraiment ___ ! This beef bourguignon is really delicious!

7. The bill is high Ce restaurant est bon, mais un peu ___ . This restaurant is good, but a little expensive.

8. A pleasant atmosphere L'ambiance ici est vraiment très ___ . The atmosphere here is really very pleasant.

9. Asking for the bill [Fin du repas, vous appelez le serveur] ___ , s'il vous plaît. The bill, please.

10. Paying by card Est-ce que vous acceptez le paiement ___ ? Do you accept payment by card?

11. No card available — cash only Je n'ai pas ma carte bancaire — je vais payer ___ . I don't have my bank card — I'm going to pay in cash.

12. Wrong order delivered Excusez-moi, ce n'est pas ___ que je veux. Excuse me, this is not what I want.

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

Focus
Épisode 4 — Le grand dîner; Épisode 5 — Bilan
Focus
Le grand dîner: food vocabulary, partitives (du, de la, des) for unspecified quantity, shopping verbs, frequency words, demonstratives (ce, cette, ces), and the futur proche.
Focus
One correct answer per question unless a page explicitly says otherwise.
Unit 4 note
Unit 4 note: this is the first explicit partitive unit for food and quantities.

Reader alignment

Characters: Wei, Amara, Mateo, Yuki, Madame Benali, Madame Sow.
Settings: boulangerie, apartment above it, classroom, La Fougère, Paris streets, market, dinner, and Bilan.
LAF1201

Unit 4 · Le grand dîner and Bilan

Épisode 4 — Le grand dîner; Épisode 5 — Bilan

PratiqueU0U1U2U3U4
Food vocabularyPartitive articlesPartitives in the negativeManger and boireFrequencyDemonstrativesFutur procheShoppingAdviceRestaurant languageDinner and Bilan capstone

Unit 4Dinner and Bilan capstone

The group prepares a real dinner for Madame Benali, neighbours, and the little family above the boulangerie, then closes with a Bilan.
Le grand dîner: food vocabulary, partitives (du, de la, des) for unspecified quantity, shopping verbs, frequency words, demonstratives (ce, cette, ces), and the futur proche.
Focus — The course closes the way it began — around a table. Le dîner d'adieu: the evening meal gets its name, you ask for cheese (du fromage), accommodate a vegetarian guest (pas DE viande), describe weekend coffee, your vegetable habit, this week's new recipe, Friday's restaurant plan, the bakery run for croissants, the cooking advice (il faut), the bill at meal's end, what you're drinking, and the new place in the neighbourhood you're all going to try. The narrative — planning the menu → cooking it → eating it → planning the next outing — gathers the entire A1 toolkit into one continuous scene, the very dinner the course has been building toward.

1. Skill 01 · Food vocabulary Le repas du soir s'appelle ___ . The evening meal is called dinner.

2. Skill 02 · Partitive articles Je voudrais ___ fromage, s'il vous plaît. I'd like some cheese, please.

3. Skill 03 · Partitive in negation Elle est végétarienne — elle ne mange pas ___ viande. She's vegetarian — she doesn't eat meat.

4. Skill 04 · Manger &amp; boire Le week-end, nous ___ souvent du café le matin. At the weekend, we often drink coffee in the morning.

5. Skill 05 · Frequency adverbs Je mange ___ des légumes — au moins une fois par jour. I always eat vegetables — at least once a day.

6. Skill 06 · Demonstrative adjectives ___ semaine, on va essayer une nouvelle recette. This week, we're going to try a new recipe.

7. Skill 07 · Futur proche Vendredi soir, on va ___ au restaurant. Friday evening, we're going to have dinner at the restaurant.

8. Skill 08 · Shopping Pour acheter des croissants, on va à la ___ . To buy croissants, you go to the bakery.

9. Skill 09 · Giving advice Pour faire cette recette, ___ préchauffer le four. To make this recipe, you need to preheat the oven.

10. Skill 10 · Au restaurant À la fin du repas, on demande : « ___ , s'il vous plaît. » The bill, please.

11. Skills 02 &amp; 04 · Integrated Qu'est-ce que tu bois ? — Je bois ___ eau minérale. I drink mineral water.

12. Skills 06 &amp; 07 · Integrated Tu connais ___ nouveau restaurant dans le quartier ? On va y aller ! Do you know this new restaurant in the neighbourhood? We're going to go!

Answer the questions, then check your answers.

Grammar card

Focus
Épisode 4 — Le grand dîner; Épisode 5 — Bilan
Focus
Le grand dîner: food vocabulary, partitives (du, de la, des) for unspecified quantity, shopping verbs, frequency words, demonstratives (ce, cette, ces), and the futur proche.
Focus
One correct answer per question unless a page explicitly says otherwise.
Unit 4 note
Unit 4 note: this is the first explicit partitive unit for food and quantities.

Reader alignment

Characters: Wei, Amara, Mateo, Yuki, Madame Benali, Madame Sow.
Settings: boulangerie, apartment above it, classroom, La Fougère, Paris streets, market, dinner, and Bilan.
